header.title: Slide Deck Mode
header.description: Write a markdown deck, preview it as slides, export a landscape PDF.

overview.intro: Slide Deck mode turns a single markdown document into a paginated 16:9 slide deck. You author the deck in markdown (often with an LLM assistant), preview it as landscape cards, and export a PPTX-style landscape PDF. DevHub never calls an LLM itself — it only defines the convention, parses it, and renders it.

rules.label: Slide separator
rules.body: A line containing only `---` ends one slide and starts the next. The split is fence-aware: a `---` inside a fenced code or mermaid block is literal content and never splits the deck.

rules.label: Per-slide config
rules.body: Each slide can open with a fenced yaml block holding its config — not `---` frontmatter (frontmatter's own `---` fences would collide with the slide separator). The yaml block is optional.

rules.label: Deck title
rules.body: There is no separate deck-level frontmatter. The first slide, with `type: title`, carries the deck's title / subtitle / author / date.

rules.label: Deck-level config
rules.body: A `type: deck` block (same yaml-fence convention, never rendered as a slide) sets deck-wide `style`/`footer` defaults. Every slide field-merges its own `style`/`footer` over these — deck = default, slide = override, one consistent model at both levels.

rules.label: Forgiving by design
rules.body: Nothing hard-fails. A chunk with no yaml, an unknown `type`, malformed yaml, or a bad style value all degrade gracefully to a rendered `content` slide — a slide is never dropped.

types.intro: Five slide types. Set with `type:` in the yaml fence.

table.body: full — lists, tables, images, code, mermaid

rules.label: content (default)
rules.body: The fallback type when `type` is missing or unrecognized. Full markdown body — mermaid diagrams and code blocks render exactly as in normal preview.

rules.label: two-column
rules.body: `columns` must be exactly 2 markdown strings. More or fewer is padded/truncated to 2 (a missing column renders empty).

fields.intro: Three universal fields work on any slide type, applied at the slide-wrapper level.

rules.label: notes
rules.body: Speaker notes. Shown in preview only, **stripped from the PDF export**.

rules.label: footer
rules.body: Per-slide footer override — flat, 2 levels: `show`, `left`, `center`, `right`, `pageNumber`. Any field you omit inherits the deck-level footer (a `type: deck` block, or the built-in default). **Page numbers are off by default** — set `footer.pageNumber: true` deck-wide or per-slide to turn them on. Common use: `footer.show: false` to hide the footer on the title slide.

rules.label: style
rules.body: Per-slide styling, nested exactly 3 levels — `style.<category>.<property>`. Categories: `background` (color, image), `title` (color, fontSize, align), `body` (color, fontSize). Unknown keys are ignored; the rest still applies.

fields.guardrailsIntro: A value that fails its rule is silently ignored and the default applies. Stay inside the rules.

guardrails.body: use `em` (relative — scales with the slide box); `px`/`rem` are accepted but stay fixed and won't scale on export. Must resolve to 8–120px.
must be an `http(s)://` URL — `javascript:`/`data:`/relative paths blocked.

overflow.label: Overflow
overflow.body: Slides are a fixed 13.333in × 7.5in box. Overlong content shrinks to fit (floor 0.6, then clips). A "content overflows" badge shows in preview only. Scale-to-fit is a safety net — keep slides short rather than relying on it.

example.description: A valid deck exercising a `type: deck` block, the title slide, universal fields, two-column, and mermaid. Toggle Slide Deck off to see the raw markdown, or paste this into the editor.
example.code:
---
```yaml
type: deck
footer:
  show: true
  left: "DevHub"
  pageNumber: true
```

---
```yaml
type: title
title: "Q3 Review"
subtitle: "Architecture & Roadmap"
author: "Aayush Gour"
date: "2026-07-14"
footer:
  show: false
style:
  background:
    color: "#0f172a"
  title:
    color: "#ffffff"
    fontSize: "2.75em"
    align: "center"
```

---
```yaml
type: content
title: "Key Points"
```
- Ships this quarter
- Mermaid theming pass
- Graph mode edge labels

---
```yaml
type: two-column
title: "Build vs Buy"
columns:
  - "### Build\n- Full control\n- Owns roadmap"
  - "### Buy\n- Faster to market\n- Vendor lock-in"
```

---
```yaml
type: content
title: "Pipeline"
```
```mermaid
graph LR
  A[Editor] --> B[Parser] --> C[Preview] --> D[PDF]
```
